title: Podman containers using an insane amount of space on fresh Debian 12 install
body:
### Issue Description

```
$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.1
API Version:  4.3.1
Go Version:   go1.19.8
Built:        Wed Dec 31 19:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```
```
$ cat /etc/subuid
user:100000:65536
```

Running Debian 12 bookworm vm inside Proxmox.

After a run of "podman system reset"

```
$ ls -al ~/.local/share/containers 
total 12
drwx------ 3 user user 4096 Aug  9 08:03 . 
drwx------ 4 user user 4096 Aug  8 13:46 .. 
drwx------ 2 user user 4096 Aug  8 13:49 cache  
```

```
~/.local/share/containers$ sudo du -hd1
188K    ./cache
192K    .
```
```
$ df -h
Filesystem      Size  Used Avail Use% Mounted on
udev             16G     0   16G   0% /dev
tmpfs           3.2G  648K  3.2G   1% /run
/dev/sda1        31G  1.4G   28G   5% /
tmpfs            16G  168K   16G   1% /dev/shm
tmpfs           5.0M     0  5.0M   0% /run/lock
log2ram         100M   30M   71M  30% /var/log
tmpfs           3.2G     0  3.2G   0% /run/user/1000

```


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. $ podman system reset

2. $ systemctl --user enable --now podman.socket

3. $ nano docker-compose.yml 
     paste in:

```
version: "3"
services:
  app:
    image: 'jc21/nginx-proxy-manager:latest'
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      # These ports are in format <host-port>:<container-port>
      - '80:80' # Public HTTP Port
      - '443:443' # Public HTTPS Port
      - '81:81' # Admin Web Port
      # Add any other Stream port you want to expose
      # - '21:21' # FTP

    # Uncomment the next line if you uncomment anything in the section
    # environment:
      # Uncomment this if you want to change the location of
      # the SQLite DB file within the container
      # DB_SQLITE_FILE: "/data/database.sqlite"

      # Uncomment this if IPv6 is not enabled on your host
      # DISABLE_IPV6: 'true'

    volumes:
      - /podman/nginx-proxy-manager/data:/data
      - /podman/nginx-proxy-manager/letsencrypt:/etc/letsencrypt
```
4.  $ podman-compose up -d
```
['podman', '--version', '']
using podman version: 4.3.1
** excluding:  set()
['podman', 'network', 'exists', 'nginx-proxy-manager_default']
['podman', 'network', 'create', '--label', 'io.podman.compose.project=nginx-proxy-manager', '--label', 'com.docker.compose.project=nginx-proxy-manager', 'nginx-proxy-manager_default']
['podman', 'network', 'exists', 'nginx-proxy-manager_default']
podman run --name=nginx-proxy-manager_app_1 -d --label io.podman.compose.config-hash=123 --label io.podman.compose.project=nginx-proxy-manager --label io.podman.compose.version=0.0.1 --label com.docker.compose.project=nginx-proxy-manager --label com.docker.compose.project.working_dir=/podman/nginx-proxy-manager --label com.docker.compose.project.config_files=docker-compose.yml --label com.docker.compose.container-number=1 --label com.docker.compose.service=app -v /podman/nginx-proxy-manager/data:/data -v /podman/nginx-proxy-manager/letsencrypt:/etc/letsencrypt --net nginx-proxy-manager_default --network-alias app -p 80:80 -p 443:443 -p 81:81 --restart unless-stopped jc21/nginx-proxy-manager:latest
Resolving "jc21/nginx-proxy-manager" using unqualified-search registries (/etc/containers/registries.conf)

### Describe the results you received

```
$ podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                      COMMAND     CREATED             STATUS                 PORTS                                           NAMES
1805868adb16  docker.io/jc21/nginx-proxy-manager:latest              About a minute ago  Up About a minute ago  0.0.0.0:80-81->80-81/tcp, 0.0.0.0:443->443/tcp  nginx-proxy-manager_app_1
```

```
$ podman images
REPOSITORY                          TAG         IMAGE ID      CREATED     SIZE
docker.io/jc21/nginx-proxy-manager  latest      4239d4258f7a  7 days ago  871 MB
```
```
$ podman system df
TYPE           TOTAL       ACTIVE      SIZE        RECLAIMABLE
Images         1           1           871.3MB     0B (0%)
Containers     1           1           35.69kB     0B (0%)
Local Volumes  0           0           0B          0B (0%)

That's over 13 GIGS for this container!!!!!



### Describe the results you expected

...WAY less storage usage.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Proxmox 8.0.4

### Additional information

I'm stumped on this one, but as of now, podman is unusable for me.

comments.body: You are using a "vfs" graphdriver rather then Overlay.  Modify the storage.conf to use overlay and reset your storage.

Thank you for the near immediate response!

I will do as you suggested and report back. 

I didn't originally modify anything in storage.conf as nothing is mentioned at https://podman.io/docs/installation , or any other place I've seen for that matter.
Debian might not be shipping a correct storage.conf,

Or shipping one at all for that matter. It seems I have zero conf files in ~/.config/containers, and no /etc/containers/storage.conf  after installing from apt.

I created the ~/.config/containers/storage.conf , and added:

```
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
rootless_storage_path = "$HOME/.local/share/containers/storage"

[storage.options]
pull_options = {enable_partial_images = "false", use_hard_links = "false", ostree_repos=""}

[storage.options.overlay]
mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
mountopt = "nodev"
```

did a reset, reinstalled the container, and now my drive is MUCH happier.

```
df -h
Filesystem      Size  Used Avail Use% Mounted on
udev             16G     0   16G   0% /dev
tmpfs           3.2G  648K  3.2G   1% /run
/dev/sda1        31G  2.7G   27G  10% /
tmpfs            16G  168K   16G   1% /dev/shm
tmpfs           5.0M     0  5.0M   0% /run/lock
log2ram         100M   31M   70M  31% /var/log
tmpfs           3.2G  132K  3.2G   1% /run/user/1000
```

I'm probably going to switch to the upstream repo as this is just in my home lab, but I'm glad this is now all documented.

I searched for a couple hours and didn't really get anywhere useful. 


Thanks again for all your help rhatdan,

Cheers

The fix was lost, when moving from Kubic to Debian:

* https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/8560
